danger_signals.visual_form: Colored enemy knight sprite moving toward player on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring collision-based combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy knight positioned above player character in vertical space
danger_signals.meaning: Player is at disadvantage in upcoming collision; defeat is likely

danger_signals.visual_form: Flat island surrounded by lava below floating platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; flying too low results in Lava Troll grab and character death

danger_signals.visual_form: Pterodactyl sprite appearing after predetermined time frame
danger_signals.meaning: Additional threat hunting the hero; bonus opportunity but adds danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Egg sprite resting on platform after enemy defeat
danger_signals.meaning: If not collected, will hatch into new enemy knight; delayed threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Groups of enemy knights with increasing visual complexity per wave
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating difficulty and pressure; world state becoming more hostile

safety_signals.visual_form: Player knight positioned above enemy knight in vertical space
safety_signals.meaning: Player advantage in collision; victory likely in engagement
Safe landing zones; allows repositioning and evasion of lava threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Vertical clearance above the lava layer
safety_signals.meaning: Safety from Lava Troll; character can fly freely without grab threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Second player character (blue knight on stork) visible on screen
safety_signals.meaning: Support for wave completion; shared threat management

safety_signals.visual_form: Egg disappearing from platform, score increment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Knight with lance mounted on ostrich
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Top-center screen position in opening screen, large bird mount distinguishes from enemies

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Knight with lance mounted on stork
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cooperates with Player 1; stork differs visually from ostrich and buzzard mounts

faction_indicators.color_identity: Specific color variant (not detailed in sources)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Separate color from other enemy types (Hunter, Shadow Lord)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Value score differs from other enemy types; wave composition

faction_indicators.color_identity: Specific color variant (not detailed in sources)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Separate color from Bounder and Shadow Lord types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distinct point value; tactical behavior in wave

faction_indicators.color_identity: Specific color variant (not detailed in sources)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Separate color identifying it as elite/final threat type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Likely appears in later waves; highest point value suggests visual prominence
Spawns after predetermined time; hunts hero; non-standard enemy archetype

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Troll creature associated with lava environment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupies lava layer below platforms; grabbing mechanic ties it to lava region

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer enemy knights on screen; simpler wave composition with Bounders or Hunters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy knights move with predictable patterns; limited threat density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased number of enemy knight sprites; mixed enemy types visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy movement becomes more aggressive; coordinated group behavior emerges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shadow Lord enemy type appears; maximum enemy density on single screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies coordinate complex attack patterns; pterodactyl adds layer of hunting behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Knight sprite removed; egg sprite appears on platform with falling trajectory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Egg falls toward bottom of screen; will hatch if not collected within time window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Egg hatches into new enemy knight sprite with new mount (buzzard)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New enemy joins active wave; threat level increases without player intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player knight sprite positioned higher on screen than enemy during engagement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collision results in enemy defeat; victorious lance is higher

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player knight sprite positioned lower than enemy during engagement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collision results in player life loss; both characters repelled if equal height

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player or enemy sprite near lava surface; Lava Troll grabbing animation triggers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character dragged into lava; immediate death for player, enemy removal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score and lives displayed at bottom platform; life counter near zero
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Next loss results in game end; UI makes remaining lives visually clear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All enemy sprites cleared from screen; transition to next wave
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New wave spawns with increased complexity; difficulty escalates visually

environmental_storytelling.example: Rock platforms float above lava; creates vertical danger gradient. Safe zone is elevated; death zone is below. Island surrounded by lava tells story of player as aerial defender protecting island space.
Player 1 rides ostrich (yellow), Player 2 rides stork (blue), enemies ride buzzards. Mount species visually communicates allegiance and role. Flying ability differentiation implied by bird choice.
Bounder, Hunter, and Shadow Lord enemies use distinct colors; visual language communicates power hierarchy without text. Shadow Lord name paired with color suggests darkness/higher threat.
Defeated enemy transforms into egg on platform, falls toward lava. Egg not collected before hatching shows consequence of incomplete victory; visual reminder of stakes.
Pterodactyl spawns on timer; visually distinct from knight+bird pairing. Bonus point opportunity but added danger; signals world becoming more hostile at specific moment.
Higher lance wins; collision height determines outcome. No hit points, no health bars—visual positioning alone tells combat result. Lance elevation is primary success metric.

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: Each completed wave introduces more enemy sprites, more complex types, more simultaneous threats. Screen becomes increasingly populated; visual density communicates rising difficulty.

environmental_storytelling.example: Characters exiting left/right reappear opposite side. Suggests bounded flying arena; world is cyclical and confined. No escape beyond screen edges.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Button mashing increases flap rate; bird gains altitude. Position on screen rises as wing input increases.
No button input allows gravity; character slowly falls. Descent rate visible through vertical position change.
Lance is positioned above enemy lance; collision will defeat enemy. Visual positioning predicts outcome.
Lance is below enemy lance; collision will cause player loss. Visual positioning predicts failure.
Character sprite proximity to lava triggers Lava Troll grab; character dragged downward into lava and removed.
Moves independently on same screen; can attack each other optionally or coordinate against waves.
Moves across screen in predictable pattern; basic AI movement on platforms. Lower threat level than other enemy types.
More aggressive movement toward player; active targeting behavior. Mid-threat level; seeks engagement.
Most aggressive movement pattern; coordinates with other enemies. High threat level; elite enemy type behavior.
Knight sprite replaced with egg sprite; egg falls toward lava. If uncollected, egg hatches into new enemy after delay.
Spawns after timer; actively pursues player character. Separate AI from knight enemies; bonus threat on timer.
Remains in lava layer; grabs any character flying too low. Indestructible; environmental hazard behavior.
Sparse enemy density; simple movement patterns. Low screen congestion; player has spatial advantage.
High enemy density; complex coordinated patterns. Screen becomes congested; evasion becomes primary challenge.

notes: Joust communicates world-state primarily through vertical positioning (collision height determines victory), entity color-coding (faction identity), and screen density escalation (wave difficulty). The game lacks explicit UI for danger beyond life counter and score display; world-state is expressed through mechanics (lava threat, egg respawning) and visual entity composition (enemy types per wave). Political situation is minimal—primarily cooperative two-player versus enemy faction with no dialogue or narrative. Safety and danger are communicated through environmental layout (platform safety, lava danger) and spatial relationships (collision positioning). NES version added graphic detail over arcade original but maintained core visual communication methods.
